info.name: Fastly Real-Time Logging API
info.description:
The Fastly Real-Time Logging API allows developers to configure and manage logging endpoints that receive streamed log data from Fastly's edge network. Fastly supports logging to a variety of formats and platforms including syslog, Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, BigQuery, Datadog, Splunk, Elasticsearch, and many other providers. Developers can create, update, and delete logging configurations per service version, controlling the format, placement, and conditions under which log entries are generated.

Contact Support:
 Name: Fastly Support

variable.description: (Required) The alphanumeric string identifying the Fastly service.
(Required) The integer identifying the service version.
(Required) The name of the syslog logging endpoint.

request.description: Retrieves the details of a specific syslog logging endpoint for a version of a Fastly service.

response.name: Successfully retrieved the syslog logging endpoint.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Unauthorized. The API token is missing or invalid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

response.name: Logging endpoint not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: The name for the real-time logging configuration.
Where in the generated VCL the logging call should be placed.
The version of the custom logging format used for the configured endpoint.
The name of an existing condition in the configured endpoint, or leave blank to always execute.
A Fastly log format string. Supports Apache Common Log variables.
A hostname or IPv4 address of the syslog endpoint.
The port number of the syslog endpoint.
Whether to use TLS for the syslog connection.
The hostname to verify the server certificate against.
A secure certificate to authenticate a server with.
The client certificate used to make authenticated requests.
The client private key used to make authenticated requests.
A token to prepend to the syslog message.
How the message should be formatted.

The S3 redundancy level.
